title: test_analyze_performance_command reads live production logs/evolution.jsonl - flaky whenever the daemon is chatting
source: Claude Fable 2026-07-07 - verify run failed this test while Ash was live-chatting with the daemon on Telegram; 15/15 pass on direct re-run minutes later
current_state: testing/test_agent_golden.py:97 test_analyze_performance_command builds a real agent and runs 'analyze performance', whose 7-day report reads the REAL logs/evolution.jsonl. The live daemon appends to that same file on every Telegram turn, so the report races concurrent writes (mid-line reads / shifting counts) and the assertions (len > 50, canonical section names) fail intermittently. Observed 2026-07-07: FAIL inside a full verify.py run at the exact time Ash was chatting with Pi at 3am; PASS 15/15 when re-run directly after. Same hygiene class as T-280 (tests touching live production state), inbound instead of outbound.
target_state: The test is hermetic: it points the evolution tracker at a temp log seeded with a handful of synthetic interaction lines (or monkeypatches the log path) so the report is deterministic regardless of daemon activity. Assertions unchanged. Test passes repeatedly while the daemon is actively writing.
migration_plan: 1. Read how _fresh_agent wires EvolutionTracker and where the log path comes from (evolution.py - likely a constructor arg or module constant).

2. In the test, point the tracker at tmp_path/'evolution.jsonl' seeded with ~5 synthetic JSONL interaction rows (timestamp within 7 days, mode, success, cost, tools_used) so the report has real content.
3. Run the test 3x in a row with the daemon running - all green.
4. python scripts/verify.py must print PASS (never pipe, T-214); SOLUTIONS.jsonl; close; refresh_pi.py.
risk_notes: testing/-only, verified root cause, reproducing conditions documented - a genuine SAFE-component candidate for sprint.py (T-263 needs a second production close; this is a natural fit after T-276). Do not 'fix' by loosening the assertions - the test's value is asserting the report renders its canonical sections.
